---
title: "TU/e Quarto Presentation Theme"
subtitle: "Reusable slide layout showcase"
author: "Ivo Roghair"
date: "18 June 2026"
format: tue-revealjs
tue-cover-image: assets/tue-bg-img1.jpg
tue-cover-logo: assets/tue-logo-white.png
# Red bar opacity (0 = fully transparent, 1 = fully opaque)
tue-cover-bar-opacity: 0.65
# Slide background colour — used for normal slides and the bottom footer strip of the cover slide
tue-slide-bg: "#f3efef" 
# Content slide text padding (top/bottom left/right), e.g. "30px 58px"
tue-content-slide-padding: "30px 58px"
# Short title shown on the left of the footer bar on every content slide
tue-short-title: "Ivo Roghair — TU/e Quarto Theme"
# Logo shown on the right of the footer bar (defaults to TU/e scarlet logo)
tue-footer-logo: "assets/tue-logo-wide-scarlet-alpha.png"
---

## Single Column Itemize

- Use this for agenda, framing, or key takeaways.
- Keep each bullet to one message.
- Use `**bold**` for emphasis and `==highlight==` for scanability.
- Replace all placeholder images at once by editing one line at the bottom:
	`[img-placeholder]: assets/rightpic.jpg`

$$
f(x) = \sum_{k=1}^{n} w_k\,\phi_k(x)
$$

## Two Columns Itemize

:::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
### Left Stream

- Problem context
- Constraints
- Required outcome
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### Right Stream

- Candidate approach
- Risks
- Validation plan
:::
::::

## Single Image

![Placeholder image. Replace by editing `[img-placeholder]` once.][img-placeholder]{fig-alt="Placeholder image" width=92%}

## Two Images

:::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
![Left placeholder image][img-placeholder]{fig-alt="Left image" width=65%}
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
![Right placeholder image][img-placeholder]{fig-alt="Right image" width=65%}
:::
::::

## Itemize Plus Image

:::: columns
::: {.column width="56%"}
### Design Notes

- Left column for reasoning.
- Right column for visual evidence.
- Good for methods, workflows, and architecture.

$$
\mathbf{y}=\mathbf{A}\mathbf{x}+\mathbf{b}
$$
:::

::: {.column width="44%"}
![Supporting figure, placement towards top of slide][img-placeholder]{fig-alt="Supporting image" width=85% style="margin-top:-15%;"}
:::
::::

## Itemize Left, Large Image Right

:::: columns
::: {.column width="38%"}
### Insights

- Short bullets only.
- Keep visual dominant.
- Large image stays above footer region.
:::

::: {.column width="62%"}
![Large right-side image][img-placeholder]{fig-alt="Large right image" width=100%}
:::
::::

## Large Image Left, Itemize Right

:::: columns
::: {.column width="62%"}
![Large left-side image][img-placeholder]{fig-alt="Large left image" width=50%}
:::

::: {.column width="38%"}
### Commentary

- Mirror layout of previous slide.
- Useful for comparison pairs.
- Keep text density low.
:::
::::

## Equation Focus

Use this pattern for derivations or compact model summaries.

$$
\begin{aligned}
\nabla \cdot (\rho \mathbf{u}) &= 0 \\
\rho\left(\frac{\partial \mathbf{u}}{\partial t} + \mathbf{u}\cdot\nabla\mathbf{u}\right) &= -\nabla p + \mu\nabla^2\mathbf{u}
\end{aligned}
$$

## Highlight Box Pattern

<div style="background:#fff3bf;border-left:8px solid #c81818;padding:0.7rem 1rem;border-radius:8px;">
<strong>Highlight:</strong> This is a reusable callout style for decisions, warnings, and main conclusions.
</div>

- Add one callout per slide maximum.
- Use it to direct audience attention.

## Closing / Next Steps

1. Start from any slide pattern in this file.
2. Replace `[img-placeholder]` with your own image path once.
3. Duplicate and adapt layouts to build your final deck quickly.

[img-placeholder]: assets/rightpic.jpg
